summary: Well-structured plan that correctly mirrors existing ConstellationLines patterns. The shader-uniform approach for bass modulation is the right call. The Overlay interface extraction is clean. Main risks are shader attribute alignment and perpendicular vector degeneracy — both manageable.

architecturalImpact.rationale: Introduces a new interface abstraction (Overlay), a new generator, a new overlay system, and a new vertex shader. Touches the central wiring in main.ts and modeManager.ts. However, the changes follow established patterns exactly (ConstellationLines is the template) and the replacement strategy avoids combinatorial complexity.

stepAssessments.title: Extract generic Overlay interface
stepAssessments.notes: Clean extraction. The ConstellationLines interface (init, draw, cleanup, setOpacity, activeVertexCount) is already the right shape. Note: Overlay.init signature takes (Scene, Float32Array, VisualParams) which differs from GeometrySystem.init(Scene, string, VisualParams) — this is intentional and correct since overlays receive positions from a parent system.

stepAssessments.title: Update ConstellationLines to implement Overlay
stepAssessments.notes: Trivial type-only change. ConstellationLines already satisfies the interface structurally.

stepAssessments.title: Update OverlayAttachment to use generic Overlay type
stepAssessments.notes: Correct approach. ModeManager only calls init/draw/cleanup/setOpacity/activeVertexCount — all on the Overlay interface. The duck-typed getPositions pattern remains unchanged.

stepAssessments.title: Create tessellateBezier generator
stepAssessments.notes: Sound algorithm. Must handle the degenerate perpendicular case (pair axis parallel to reference vector). Existing generators like subdivideEdges return similar structures (positions, aEdgeParam, aRandom) — follow that pattern exactly. The plan says 'segments * 2 for LineSegments' which is correct (each segment needs start+end vertex). Ensure aArcOffset (vec3 per vertex) is also returned here, not computed later.

stepAssessments.title: Create BezierCurveWeb system
stepAssessments.notes: The plan correctly identifies two approaches for bass modulation and selects the shader-uniform path (b). Critical detail: the plan must store flat (straight-line) positions in the position attribute and arc offsets in aArcOffset, then the vertex shader displaces. This means the geometry is built once at init() and never rebuilt — matching the ConstellationLines pattern. Connection finding should reuse the existing findConnections algorithm from constellationLines.ts (extract it or duplicate it). Current findConnections is O(n²) bounded by maxConnections — acceptable for 2000 connections.

stepAssessments.title: Add Bezier vertex shader
stepAssessments.notes: The sin(aEdgeParam * PI) bell-curve weighting is elegant — zero displacement at endpoints, maximum at midpoint. Must declare ALL attributes used: position (built-in), aArcOffset (vec3), aEdgeParam (float), aDistance (float), aRandom (vec3). Must declare ALL uniforms: uBassArcScale plus all existing constellation uniforms. The plan says to reuse constellation.frag.glsl — this works since the fragment shader only reads vAlpha and vDepth varyings. Ensure the new vert shader outputs these same varyings.

stepAssessments.title: Add quality tier fields
stepAssessments.notes: Three new fields (enableBezierWeb, maxBezierConnections, bezierSegments) is clean. Consider: since this replaces constellation lines, should enableConstellationLines be set to false when enableBezierWeb is true? Or leave both fields independent for future flexibility? The plan's tier values (low=disabled, medium=800/4, high=2000/6) are reasonable. 2000 connections * 6 segments * 2 verts = 24K vertices — well within GPU budget for LineSegments.

stepAssessments.title: Wire into main.ts
stepAssessments.notes: The replacement strategy is correct per ticket intent. Simple swap: replace createConstellationLines() call with createBezierCurveWeb(). The attachOverlay pattern and getPositions duck-typing remain identical. Should guard with quality.enableBezierWeb instead of quality.enableConstellationLines. Consider: keep both quality flags so constellation can still be activated independently in future tickets.

stepAssessments.title: Write unit tests
stepAssessments.notes: Test placement under tests/ matches project convention. The test plan mirrors constellationLines.test.ts patterns (T-055 prefix → T-067 prefix). Key test cases to include: vertex count = connections * segments * 2, arc offset perpendicularity, endpoints match input positions, bass modulation uniform propagation.

stepAssessments.title: Build verification
stepAssessments.notes: Standard definition-of-done gate. The shader compilation check is especially important given the new vertex shader.

risks.mitigation: Checklist: every attribute in the vertex shader (aArcOffset, aEdgeParam, aDistance, aRandom) must have a corresponding BufferAttribute set on the geometry before first render. The plan accounts for this but implementation must be exact.

When pair axis is near-parallel to the reference vector (e.g., (0,1,0)), the cross product approaches zero. Use a fallback: if |cross| < epsilon, use an alternative reference vector. The existing codebase handles similar cases in edge tangent computation.

The plan doesn't explicitly state whether findConnections is extracted as shared utility or duplicated. Should be extracted into a shared module (e.g., src/visual/generators/findConnections.ts) to avoid drift between constellation and bezier implementations.
Since ConstellationLines already structurally satisfies Overlay, the extraction is purely additive. No runtime behavior changes.

missingConsiderations: The plan does not address extracting findConnections as a shared utility — it's currently private inside constellationLines.ts
No mention of whether enableConstellationLines quality field should be deprecated or coexist with enableBezierWeb

The plan reuses constellation.frag.glsl but the fragment shader references vArcDisplacement in the electric arc variant — ensure the standard (non-arc) fragment shader is used, which only needs vAlpha and vDepth

performanceBudget.cpuPerFrame: Negligible — geometry built once at init, only uniform updates per frame
performanceBudget.gpuImpact: Comparable to existing constellation lines — same fragment shader, slightly more complex vertex shader (one extra attribute read + sin() call)

recommendations: Extract findConnections from constellationLines.ts into a shared generator module before step 5
Add a baseArcHeight config parameter (default ~0.3) so the perpendicular offset has a sensible baseline before bass modulation
In the vertex shader, clamp uBassArcScale to prevent arc inversion at extreme bass values (e.g., max 3.0)
Keep enableConstellationLines and enableBezierWeb as independent quality flags — future tickets may want both or neither
